options.content: **eff batch** = `worker_rows_served_total / worker_dispatch_total` = the average **rows per device execution**, i.e. the true effective batch size. This counts rows that arrived pre-batched inside a single request (client-side batching) as well as rows the server coalesced, so it is the real answer to "are we batching?". **req-merge** = `worker_requests_served_total / worker_dispatch_total` = how many *separate* requests the server merged per execution; it stays ~1 when the client pre-batches or when there's no concurrency, even if eff batch is large. **max batch** = the compiled ceiling; **fill** = eff batch / max batch (how full the batch dimension runs). **device s/s** = device-seconds per wall-second (1.0 = one full device). So: high eff batch + req-merge 1 = the client is batching and the server isn't merging (fine); eff batch 1 + low fill on a batchable model = genuinely unbatched (opportunity). Aggregated over the selected range and worker(s); covers every device-executing model incl. meta sub-models.

panels.title: Per-model batching & device cost (sorted by device usage)
panels.description: eff batch (rows/dispatch) is the real batch size; req-merge (requests/dispatch) is server-side merging only. A batchable model (max batch > 1) running eff batch ~1 with low fill is unbatched; high eff batch with req-merge 1 means the client pre-batches. Sorted by device s/s; click any header to re-sort. Models with no dispatches in the range are excluded.

targets.expr: sum by (model) (rate(worker_rows_served_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])), 1e-9) and on (model) (sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) > 0)

sum by (model) (rate(worker_requests_served_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])), 1e-9) and on (model) (sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) > 0)

(sum by (model) (rate(worker_rows_served_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])), 1e-9)) / clamp_min(max by (model) (worker_model_max_batch_size{worker=~"$worker"}), 1) and on (model) (sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) > 0)

sum by (model) (rate(worker_compute_seconds_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])), 1e-9) and on (model) (sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) > 0)

panels.title: Most under-filled batchable models (bottom 20 by fill)
panels.description: The 20 models (max batch > 1) whose effective batch runs furthest below their compiled ceiling , the clearest batching-improvement candidates. Fill near 100% is already well-batched (by client or server). Uses rows/dispatch, so client-side batching counts.

targets.expr: bottomk(20, (sum by (model) (rate(worker_rows_served_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])) / clamp_min(sum by (model) (rate(worker_dispatch_total{worker=~"$worker"}[$__range])), 1e-9)) / clamp_min(max by (model) (worker_model_max_batch_size{worker=~"$worker"}), 1) and on (model) (max by (model) (worker_model_max_batch_size{worker=~"$worker"}) > 1))
